def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    # Calculate Bollinger Bands
    bb = ta.volatility.BollingerBands(df['close'], window=params['bb_window'], window_dev=params['bb_dev'])
    df['bb_upper'] = bb.bollinger_hband()
    df['bb_lower'] = bb.bollinger_lband()
    
    # Calculate Keltner Channel
    kc = ta.volatility.KeltnerChannel(
        high=df['high'],
        low=df['low'],
        close=df['close'],
        window=params['kc_window'],
        window_atr=params['kc_atr_window'],
        multiplier=params['kc_multiplier']
    )
    df['kc_upper'] = kc.keltner_channel_hband()
    df['kc_lower'] = kc.keltner_channel_lband()
    
    # Signal generation
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    
    # Long when Bollinger Band width is below threshold and price breaks above Keltner Channel
    long_condition = (
        (df['close'] > df['kc_upper']) &
        ((df['bb_upper'] - df['bb_lower']) / df['close'] < params['volatility_threshold'])
    )
    
    # Short when Bollinger Band width is below threshold and price breaks below Keltner Channel
    short_condition = (
        (df['close'] < df['kc_lower']) &
        ((df['bb_upper'] - df['bb_lower']) / df['close'] < params['volatility_threshold'])
    )
    
    signals.loc[long_condition] = 1
    signals.loc[short_condition] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/JPY exhibits strong directional moves following periods of volatility compression, identified by Bollinger Band width contraction combined with Keltner Channel breakouts.
